metrics.narrative: PIF/Efficio's stated objective is automating manual procurement processes and deploying AI automation (MEDDPICC CRM field, last updated 6 Apr 2026, ~3 months ago). No buyer-voiced quantified metric (cost, cycle time, headcount) has been captured in any call, email, or task in the record. The demo scope on file (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, Merlin Intake) reflects the intended breadth of the business case but has not been corroborated with a fresh buyer quote in the last 90 days.

champion.narrative: Andrew Waterhouse (PIF, Shared Services Division / Procurement Department) is the longest-standing and warmest contact, with a cordial relationship documented back to at least 22 Apr 2025 and continuing through 18 Jun 2026 (~3 weeks ago), when he welcomed a reconnect and offered an in-person meeting at PIF's Riyadh (KAFD) office. Four days later (22 Jun 2026), however, he pushed back on a parallel, uncoordinated Zycus outreach that had cc'd the SVP directly, stating 'for the time being we do not need further review of the Zycus offering' and asking that communication run through a single channel. No evidence in the record shows him advocating internally for Zycus — this reads as cordial gatekeeping, not active championing, consistent with the CRM's own original assessment that he would be 'ultra-professional' and hard to convert 'well into the late RFP stage.'

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua and JAGGAER are all named as expected participants once PIF's formal RFP launches ('at least all other MQ Leaders,' MEDDPICC CRM field, 6 Apr 2026). Efficio, the consulting firm running the process, explicitly declined to be influenced toward Zycus on 20 Mar 2026 ('not interested, Efficio are vendor agnostic'), removing any process lever. No head-to-head evidence exists yet since the RFP has not issued, but Zycus's own noted gaps in market intelligence and category management are a specific exposure versus GEP Worldwide, which per the sales playbook competes directly on managed-services and category depth.

identify_pain.narrative: The stated pain is automating manual procurement processes and deploying AI automation across a full S2P scope (MEDDPICC CRM field, 6 Apr 2026). Per Salesforce's own AIS_Why__c assessment, the Merlin Intake focus in the demo scope is rep-positioned rather than buyer-articulated, and no buyer quote in the record confirms AI specifically as an evaluation driver — the buyer-side conversation has centered on KSA hosting requirements and Efficio's vendor-agnostic posture instead.

paper_process.narrative: No commercial or contracting motion exists; the deal is pre-RFP. A structural blocker has been flagged repeatedly since 8 Oct 2025 (~9 months ago): PIF's exercise will require KSA local hosting and a legal entity or regional office presence, which Zycus does not currently have, and this remains an open, unresolved ask in the current Next Step ('confirm KSA local-hosting commitment & provisional timeline').

economic_buyer.narrative: The deal team has identified Abdulaziz Alsuwailem, SVP, as the economic buyer and attempted a direct approach via email on 18 and 22 June 2026 (cc'ing him directly). He has never personally responded; all engagement continues to run through Andrew Waterhouse, who on 22 June 2026 (~2.5 weeks ago) asked that all Zycus communication be consolidated through a single channel (the deal owner and the deal owner's manager) rather than reach him or other PIF staff directly. The earlier CRM-recorded EB chain (Efficio consultants and a project lead, updated 6 Apr 2026) is superseded by this more recent, more specific target.

decision_process.narrative: The process has followed a clear, well-evidenced sequence over roughly 14 months: registration, NDA and code-of-conduct submission (12 Jan 2026, ~6 months ago), a registration-access delay resolved by 7 Feb 2026 (~5 months ago), RFI received 2 Mar 2026 and submitted 15 Mar 2026 (~4 months ago), and a stated intent to move to formal RFP once PIF's internal exercise formally launches — originally targeted for 'after Eid, end March 2026' (per an 8 Oct 2025 note), later slipped to 'Q2' (13 May 2026), and still not issued as of today (9 Jul 2026). This sequence is corroborated across Next_Step_History__c and completed Tasks.

decision_criteria.narrative: PIF's process is understood to be a formal RFP and negotiation exercise across 'all top-tier providers,' covering the full S2P scope (MEDDPICC CRM field, 6 Apr 2026), with an internally-flagged gap in market intelligence and category management that could weigh against Zycus once formal scoring begins. No RFP document exists yet, so specific weighted criteria remain directional, not confirmed.

items.name: Andy shuts down Brien's follow-up, says no review needed now
items.summary: Responding to Brien's request for a 30-min working session on PIF's S2C initiative, Andy Waterhouse told him to stop copying departed PIF staff and route all communication through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him, adding that PIF does not need further review of the Zycus offering at this time — a clear stall signal on the deal.

items.name: Carl apologizes and confirms single-channel comms
items.summary: Carl Kimball quickly responded to Andy's rebuke, apologizing and confirming that all communications will now go through Hakim and himself, and that the internal team has been updated — an attempt to repair the relationship and unblock further engagement.

items.name: Andy acknowledges Carl's correction
items.summary: Andy sent a brief one-line reply thanking Carl for confirming the consolidated communication channel, closing the loop on the process issue for now.

items.name: Andy resurfaces after holiday, asks for single channel and KAFD visit
items.summary: In an earlier reply on the same thread, Andy explained he'd been on holiday, noted he was also getting separate emails from Brien Rodrigues and asked Carl to consolidate to one channel, while inviting Carl to propose dates to meet in person at KAFD — the root cause of the day's channel-cleanup exchange.

day_summary.overall: A messy multi-thread email exchange forced a hard reset on how Zycus engages PIF: Andy Waterhouse pushed back on Brien's outreach, pointing out several cc'd contacts have left PIF and stating there's no need for further review of the Zycus offering right now, while insisting all communication be consolidated through Carl, Dan and Hakim to him directly. Carl quickly conceded the point and confirmed the team will now route everything through Hakim and himself, and separately worked to set up an in-person meeting with Andy in KAFD. Net effect: the deal's active review is paused/stalled per the buyer, and the priority has shifted from pitching capabilities to first fixing the channel and re-earning a working session.

cro_panel.intro: This is a pre-RFP, partner/consultant-mediated enterprise pursuit stuck for months awaiting PIF's formal RFP launch.; The relationship with PIF's procurement lead is warm and long-running but shows no evidence of active advocacy, the economic buyer has never personally engaged despite a direct approach in June 2026, and a recent internal coordination lapse (three uncoordinated Zycus contacts reaching PIF within days) created real friction.; The close date is not defensible against the evidence
